heading: Policyholder dividends deduction
label: 808 Policyholder dividends deduction
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1984, referred to in subsec. (f)(4), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 98–369, div. AJuly 18, 1984

children.heading: Policyholder dividend defined
children.label: a Policyholder dividend defined
children.text: For purposes of this part, the term “policyholder dividend” means any dividend or similar distribution to policyholders in their capacity as such.
any amount paid or credited (including as an increase in benefits) where the amount is not fixed in the contract but depends on the experience of the company or the discretion of the management,
excess interest,
premium adjustments, and
experience-rated refunds.

children.heading: Amount of deduction
children.label: c Amount of deduction
children.text: The deduction for policyholder dividends for any taxable year shall be an amount equal to the policyholder dividends paid or accrued during the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: paid or credited to a policyholder in his capacity as such, and

children.label: B
children.text: in excess of interest determined at the prevailing State assumed rate for such contract.

children.heading: Premium adjustment
children.label: 2 Premium adjustment
children.text: The term “premium adjustment” means any reduction in the premium under an insurance or annuity contract which (but for the reduction) would have been required to be paid under the contract.

children.heading: Experience-rated refund
children.label: 3 Experience-rated refund
children.text: The term “experience-rated refund” means any refund or credit based on the experience of the contract or group involved.
increases the cash surrender value of the contract or other benefits payable under the contract, or
reduces the premium otherwise required to be paid,

children.heading: Coordination of 1984 fresh-start adjustment with acceleration of policyholder dividends deduction through change in business practice
children.label: f Coordination of 1984 fresh-start adjustment with acceleration of policyholder dividends deduction through change in business practice

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount determined under paragraph (1) of subsection (c) for the year of change shall (before any reduction under paragraph (2) of subsection (c)) be reduced by so much of the accelerated policyholder dividends deduction for such year as does not exceed the 1984 fresh-start adjustment for policyholder dividends (to the extent such adjustment was not previously taken into account under this subsection).

children.heading: Year of change
children.label: 2 Year of change
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “year of change” means the taxable year in which the change in business practices which results in the accelerated policyholder dividends deduction takes effect.

children.heading: Accelerated policyholder dividends deduction defined
children.label: 3 Accelerated policyholder dividends deduction defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “accelerated policyholder dividends deduction” means the amount which (but for this subsection) would be determined for the taxable year under paragraph (1) of subsection (c) but which would have been determined (under such paragraph) for a later taxable year under the business practices of the taxpayer as in effect at the close of the preceding taxable year.

children.heading: 1984 fresh-start adjustment for policyholder dividends
children.label: 4 1984 fresh-start adjustment for policyholder dividends
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “1984 fresh-start adjustment for policyholder dividends” means the amounts held as of , by the taxpayer as reserves for dividends to policyholders under section 811(b) (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1984) other than for dividends which accrued before . Such amounts shall be properly reduced to reflect the amount of previously nondeductible policyholder dividends (as determined under section 809(f) as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1984).December 31, 1983January 1, 1984

children.heading: Separate application with respect to lines of business
children.label: 5 Separate application with respect to lines of business
children.text: This subsection shall be applied separately with respect to each line of business of the taxpayer.

children.heading: Subsection not to apply to mere change in dividend amount
children.label: 6 Subsection not to apply to mere change in dividend amount
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to a mere change in the amount of policyholder dividends.

children.heading: Subsection not to apply to policies issued afterDecember 31, 1983
children.label: 7 Subsection not to apply to policies issued afterDecember 31, 1983

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any policyholder dividend paid or accrued with respect to a policy issued after .December 31, 1983

children.heading: Exchanges of substantially similar policies
children.label: B Exchanges of substantially similar policies
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), any policy issued after , in exchange for a substantially similar policy issued on or before such date shall be treated as issued before . A similar rule shall apply in the case of a series of exchanges.December 31, 1983January 1, 1984

children.heading: Subsection to apply to policies provided under employee benefit plans
children.label: 8 Subsection to apply to policies provided under employee benefit plans
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any policyholder dividend paid or accrued with respect to a group policy issued in connection with a plan to provide welfare benefits to employees (within the meaning of section 419(e)(2)).

children.heading: Prevailing State assumed interest rate
children.label: g Prevailing State assumed interest rate

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “prevailing State assumed interest rate” means, with respect to any contract, the highest assumed interest rate permitted to be used in computing life insurance reserves for insurance contracts or annuity contracts (as the case may be) under the insurance laws of at least 26 States. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the effect of nonforfeiture laws of a State on interest rates for reserves shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: When rate determined
children.label: 2 When rate determined
children.text: The prevailing State assumed interest rate with respect to any contract shall be determined as of the beginning of the calendar year in which the contract was issued.
